message:
feat(lax,trace): use rfind for FFT length parsing and add split axis-default test

- crates/fj-lax/src/fft.rs: replace `.filter(...).next_back()` with
  `.rfind(...)` to find the last non-empty segment when parsing optional
  FFT lengths; this is more idiomatic and avoids a redundant intermediate
  iterator step

- crates/fj-trace/src/lib.rs: add new test
  `test_infer_split_shapes_defaults_axis_to_zero` that verifies the Split
  primitive correctly defaults its axis parameter to zero when none is
  supplied, asserting output shapes and dtypes after processing a 1-D
  F64 vector of length 8 split into 4 sections of size 2

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -81,8 +81,7 @@ fn parse_optional_fft_length(
         let len = raw
             .split(',')
             .map(str::trim)
-            .filter(|part| !part.is_empty())
-            .next_back()
+            .rfind(|part| !part.is_empty())
             .ok_or_else(|| EvalError::Unsupported {
                 primitive,
                 detail: format!("invalid fft_lengths: {raw}"),
